title: Jira Automation Rule Loop or Comment Storm Detection
app: Splunk Add-on for Jira Cloud audit input
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="jira:cloud:audit:log" OR sourcetype="jira:audit"
| search action="commented" OR method="comment"
| stats count by issue_key, actor
| where count>30
| sort -count
description: Runaway automation spams tickets and hides real human updates.
value: Catches feedback loops after bad JQL in Automation rules.
implementation: Throttle audit polling; correlate with rule deployment changes.

references.title: Source types — Splunk Add-on for Jira Cloud

knownFalsePositives: Bursts of comments and automation from integrations, copy-paste from other tools, or rule changes during an incident; tune the rule and exclude known bots with care.
grandmaExplanation: We look for a rule or a comment loop that is running away, so automation helps instead of filling the work item with noise.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure: Splunk Add-on for Jira Cloud audit input.
• Data: Jira audit feed (`jira:cloud:audit:log`).
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Throttle audit polling; correlate with rule deployment changes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="jira:cloud:audit:log" OR sourcetype="jira:audit"
| search action="commented" OR method="comment"
| stats count by issue_key, actor
| where count>30
| sort -count
```

Understanding this search
Runaway automation spams tickets and hides real human updates.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the Jira issue page, board, or service project view for the same dates. Confirm issue keys, status, and assignment fields; reconcile timestamp fields you use in the report.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Timeline (comments per minute), Table (issue × actor).
